text: Injuries and lack of motivation are the most common reasons for discontinuation of running.
Real-time feedback from wearables can reduce running discontinuation by reducing injury risk and improving both performance and motivation.
Feedback targeted to an individual's specific reasons for running can improve motivation and compliance with training.
A combination of feedback modalities (e.g., visual, auditory, haptic) and a mixture of real-time and delayed feedback is more effective than any single modality or timing alone.
The most effective feedback modality and frequency differ between variables being monitored and between individuals, requiring personalization.
Feedback that promotes perceived competence, autonomy, and an external focus of attention can improve motivation, motor learning, and performance in runners.
Real-time feedback on both running technique and running workload can be applied to reduce injury risk and improve performance.
